job.title: Evening Concierge
job.description: At Bozzuto, every team member shares a deep commitment to doing good for those around us . We live this each day by designing, building, managing and maintaining one-of-a-kind residences. Whether it’s the talent within our communities or the expertise across our property operations teams, we work together to make extraordinary happen for our clients, residents and fellow team members. Our people are dedicated to delivering exceptional experiences to everyone we serve including each other. This s

job.title: Assistant General Manager
job.description:
Bozzuto's Assistant General Manager role in Annapolis sits squarely between the leasing floor and the GM's office. You're not purely a closer, and you're not purely an ops person. You do both, often on the same afternoon. That duality is the whole point of the job.

On the leasing side, you're conducting tours that actually convert. Not scripted walkthroughs, but conversations that help a prospective resident picture their life in the community. Traffic-to-lease ratios matter here. So does retention. You're working to make sure every resident interaction lands well enough that renewals feel like an easy yes rather than a negotiation.

On the financial side, you're in the rent collection process daily. Posting receipts, watching delinquency, keeping accounts receivable tight. You don't need to be an accountant, but you do need to understand why a delinquency spike at month-end is everyone's problem and how to address it before it becomes the GM's headache.

When the GM is out, you're running the show. That means coaching the team, keeping the property's interior and exterior presentation sharp, and solving resident issues without escalating everything upward. The ability to make a confident call without backup is something Bozzuto is specifically looking for here.

Weekend availability is part of the deal. One to two weekends per month, with weekday days off in exchange. That's when prospective residents are actually out looking, so it's when the leasing work gets done. It's not unusual in this business, but it's worth knowing upfront.

What separates strong AGM candidates from average ones at a company like Bozzuto isn't just sales ability. It's the combination of interpersonal range and operational discipline. Plenty of people can give a good tour. Fewer can give a great tour and then go back to the office and clean up a delinquency report without losing momentum. That combination is what this role actually demands.

What you bring

  • Four-year degree or equivalent experience in property management, hospitality, or retail
  • At least two years in a client-facing role with some exposure to rent collection, budgets, or accounts receivable
  • Comfort with Yardi or comparable property management software
  • Active presence on social platforms and the ability to create content that reflects a community's character
  • Strong written and verbal communication, and the professional polish to represent the brand on-site

The salary range runs from roughly $72,600 to $75,000, with bonus eligibility on top of that. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement. For someone looking to grow toward a General Manager seat, this role is a direct path. Bozzuto promotes from within, and the AGM position is exactly where that track starts.

job.title: Assistant Maintenance Manager
job.description:
Maintenance operations at multifamily properties only work when someone bridges the gap between hands-on trade work and team coordination. That's what this role does. Bozzuto is hiring an Assistant Maintenance Manager at their Atlanta property to keep the building running, the turns moving, and the team accountable.

What the Job Actually Looks Like

You're splitting your time between wrench work and oversight. On any given day, that means diagnosing an HVAC issue in one unit, checking punch list progress on a make-ready down the hall, then following up on a resident service ticket that's been open too long. You're not just fixing things. You're making sure the whole maintenance operation holds its standard.

The role covers the full trades spectrum: HVAC, plumbing, electrical, appliance repair, basic carpentry, drywall, and painting. Exposure to complex mechanical systems like central plant, chillers, or boilers is a plus. Preventative maintenance programs are yours to execute consistently, not just check off a list.

The make-ready process is a big piece of this. Turns need to move fast and finish clean so leasing can do its job. You'll manage that flow with a sharp eye for detail and a real sense of urgency.

On-call rotation is part of the deal. Weekends too. That's the job, and strong candidates go in knowing it.

What Bozzuto Needs From You

  • 5 or more years in the trades, facilities, or residential building maintenance
  • HVAC certification, EPA certification, or NAPE certification (at least one)
  • Solid working knowledge of OSHA, MSDS, and risk management basics
  • Some supervisory experience, or a clear track record of leading peers informally
  • Comfort with property management technology platforms and basic Microsoft Office tools
  • Physical capability to handle the demands of the role: lifting up to 50 pounds frequently, climbing ladders, working outdoors in varying conditions

Pay runs $27 to $29 per hour, with bonus eligibility. Benefits include medical, dental, and vision coverage, 20 days of paid time off plus holidays, a 401k with company match, and tuition reimbursement.

The candidates who stand out in this role aren't just technically sharp. They're the ones who treat a resident's work order with the same seriousness they bring to a boiler inspection. That combination of trade skill and people accountability is what separates a good maintenance tech from someone ready to step into a leadership track. From here, the natural next move is a Service Manager or Maintenance Director role, and Bozzuto's size means those opportunities exist within the same portfolio.
